acceptance_criteria: Spreadsheet openable and parseable by openpyxl with visible formulas in the Calc and Checks sheets
Inputs & Provenance sheet maps all derived outputs to embedded-packet sources
Cost-consequence table provides per-record dollar estimates and a budget comparison
No contradictory passed/failed rubric metadata in grader/verifier outputs
extra_model_fields.estimated_effort: Small (update workbook, add provenance sheet, attach script and screenshots) - expect a few hours of focused work.
expected_outcomes: comparability_matrix.xlsx is parseable and shows live formulas for priority and breach checks
control_strategy.md references workbook sheet names and cell ranges for all key reported numbers
explicit cost mapping to decision_budget_usd is present and justifies recommendations
reproducible script verifies calculations independent of spreadsheet parsing
extra_model_fields.notes: If inability to install openpyxl persists in verifier environment, include formula-view screenshots and the reproducible script to ensure verifiability without depending on spreadsheet parsing.

planned_changes: Rebuild comparability_matrix.xlsx ensuring all calculation cells contain live formulas (no pasted values) and include named sheets: Packet, Calc, Checks, Inputs & Provenance, Limits, Summary.

Add an 'Inputs & Provenance' sheet that maps each derived output cell (priority_value, breach flags, +5% sensitivity values, economic proxy) to the original embedded-packet fields (task.json/task_brief.md) with explicit cell coordinates and source file lines/keys.

Add a 'Limits' sheet exposing limit values (attribute_shift_sd limit, clinical_relevance limit, method_precision limit) and update validation formulas to reference these cells rather than hardcoded constants.

Include a cost-consequence worksheet and a summary table in control_strategy.md with dollar estimates (or defensible ranges) per-record for recommended mitigations and a subtotal compared to decision_budget_usd (295000). If projected spend > budget, provide a prioritized/staged mitigation plan.

Produce a small reproducible script (Python script or Jupyter notebook) that reads the embedded packet and recomputes priority and sensitivity outputs; include exact inputs and any seeds used. Attach this script to the deliverables.

If environment parsing remains unreliable for reviewers, include a formula-view export: CSV of key formula cells and PNG screenshots of formula-view for critical worksheets (Calc, Checks, Inputs & Provenance).

Correct grader/verifier metadata inconsistencies prior to submission: ensure passed_criteria and failed_criteria are consistent across top-level and nested metadata fields and add a short metadata note documenting any residual open items.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Address workbook parseability/provenance gaps, supply explicit cost-consequence mapping tied to decision_budget_usd, and fix internal metadata inconsistencies reported by verifier.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline deliverables are present and the decision rationale is documented in control_strategy.md. The attempt passes artifact-contract checks, but there are three substantive issues to address before full verification: (1) the workbook (.xlsx) could not be programmatically parsed in this environment (openpyxl_unavailable), leaving formulas/provenance unverified; (2) cost-consequence mapping to the declared decision_budget_usd is missing (rubric item ri_3 scored low); and (3) internal metadata inconsistency across grader outputs (passed/failed criteria lists) should be normalized. Fixes should include a parseable workbook or formula export, explicit Inputs & Provenance mapping in the workbook, explicit dollar cost estimates tied to decision_budget_usd and a short mitigation/budget plan, and correction of inconsistent metadata.
